methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
SpaceX is a late-stage private company in the Space & Aerospace sector preparing for IPO, having raised $10B+ against a current valuation of $800B. The comparable universe consists of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies including Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, and Sierra Space.

Traditional valuation triangulation methods face material constraints here. Peer-median analysis is not applicable: while 24 same-sector peers exist, filtering for stage, adjacency, or valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, making median-based inference unreliable. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples similarly cannot be executed—the public Space & Aerospace cohort trades at a median 42.9x P/S across six tickers, but SpaceX's revenue is not disclosed in available data. Applying public multiples to an undisclosed revenue base would be speculative.

The $800B valuation cannot be independently validated through the standard triangulation toolkit given these data gaps. Any meaningful assessment would require either: (1) disclosed or estimated revenue figures to anchor public-comp multiples; or (2) a sufficiently dense set of comparably-staged private transactions. The analysis is therefore constrained to noting the composition of the comp set rather than deriving a supported range. Key uncertainties center on revenue scale and profitability trajectory relative to public-market precedent.
